title: Add idempotency to an existing operation
description: Introduce an idempotency key and dedup store so a repeated request produces one effect and a stable response.
body: Steps: 1. Identify the operation's natural uniqueness (client key, order id) and decide the key. 2. Add a dedup record (unique index on the key) written in the same transaction as the effect. 3. On conflict, return the stored prior result instead of re-running. 4. Add a replay test issuing the same key twice and asserting one effect. 5. Backfill/expire keys with a TTL policy. Rollback point: before enabling the unique constraint in production. Validation: concurrent duplicate requests resolve to exactly one effect, confirmed by the replay and a race test.
